data.title: Webjet Bull Stays Positive Despite Marketing Costs — Market Talk
data.description: Webjet's bull at Wilsons stays positive on the stock despite the Australian travel agent flagging increased spending on marketing. Analyst Ben Wilson tells clients in a note, that Webjet's ambition to double its annual transaction value by fiscal 2030 comes at a significant cost. In addition to expenditure on a brand relaunch, he points to a gradual moderation in revenue as a proportion of transaction value. However, the stock is still cheap compared to peers, trading at three times Wilsons' fiscal 2026 Ebitda forecast. Wilsons cuts its target price by 7.2% to A$0.90 and keeps an overweight rating on the stock. Shares are flat at A$0.56. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0337 GMT - Webjet's bull at Wilsons stays positive on the stock despite the Australian travel agent flagging increased spending on marketing. Analyst Ben Wilson tells clients in a note, that Webjet's ambition to double its annual transaction value by fiscal 2030 comes at a significant cost. In addition to expenditure on a brand relaunch, he points to a gradual moderation in revenue as a proportion of transaction value. However, the stock is still cheap compared to peers, trading at three times Wilsons' fiscal 2026 Ebitda forecast. Wilsons cuts its target price by 7.2% to A$0.90 and keeps an overweight rating on the stock. Shares are flat at A$0.56. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0324 GMT - IOI's fiscal 3Q net profit could improve sequentially as fair-value losses on derivatives reverse, AmInvestment Bank's Gan Huey Ling says in a note. Manufacturing EBIT is set to rise, with higher selling prices for oleochemical products offsetting rising palm kernel oil costs, the analyst says. However, refining margins could remain under pressure due to Indonesian competition, she says. Plantation earnings are likely to fall on quarter due to a projected 20%-25% drop in fresh-fruit-bunch production, she adds. Gan expects IOI's FY 2025 crude palm oil production costs to be flat at 2,500 ringgit a ton, with lower fertilizer costs offset by higher wages. AmInvestment Bank maintains a buy rating on IOI with a target price of MYR5.00. Shares are 0.5% lower at MYR3.72. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0321 GMT - Singapore Technologies Engineering's 2029 targets imply steady long-term growth, RHB Research's Shekhar Jaiswal says in a research report. The technology, defense and engineering group's successful achievements of its previous five-year targets suggest it can attain its 8.6% revenue and up to 13.6% profit CAGR targets for 2024-2029, the analyst says. Its Commercial Aerospace segment is expected to reach S$6.0 billion revenue by 2029, thanks to factors including investments in next-generation capabilities in composite aerostructures, the analyst says. Its Defence & Public Security segment could deliver over S$7.5 billion revenue by 2029, partly due to expanding its international defence business. RHB raises the stock's target price to S$7.80 from S$5.90 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 5.2% higher at S$6.91. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0245 GMT - Coway stands to benefit from its stable domestic operation and continuing overseas expansion, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. The appliance rental company leads South Korea's market with 10.3 million rental accounts as of 2024, the analysts note. That gives Coway a competitive moat, enabling it to offset initial negative cash flow from upfront costs and to effectively capitalize on future cash flow, the analysts say. Daiwa forecasts Coway's operating profit to rise 6% in 2025 and 13% in 2026, partly driven by improved earnings from its overseas business. They initiate stock coverage with an outperform rating and a target price of KRW95,000. Shares are 6.15% higher at KRW89,800. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0236 GMT - Tencent could further expand its user base by introducing AI solutions to various consumer-facing products, Citi analysts say in a research note. As constraints over graphic processing units gradually ease, following its stepped-up capital expenditure investment, Tencent's cloud revenue could accelerate in 2025, the analysts say. Citi raises Tencent's 2025 revenue and earnings estimates by 3.3% and 7.7%, respectively, reflecting the company's latest results. Citi maintains a buy rating on Tencent and raises its target price to HK$681.00 from HK$648.00, citing its solid execution track record, steady gaming profit, and likely rising revenue from its ad and business services, boosted its AI adoption. Shares are last down 2.4% at HK$527.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - Malaysian consumer sector growth could be positive in 2025, driven by wage hikes, robust economic conditions, and government subsidies supporting lower-income groups, RHB IB analysts Soong Wei Siang and Tai Yu Jie say in a note. This trend appears to be unfolding, with most companies spoken to experiencing positive momentum in their quarterly numbers, they say. However, inflation risks from petrol subsidy reforms may remain uncertain, they note. RHB maintains a neutral rating on Malaysia's consumer products sector, and peg Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M), Guan Chong, Farm Fresh and Focus Point as top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0211 GMT - Tencent could become a dominant AI player in China given its unparalleled breadth and depth of social and user insight, Barclays analysts say in a research note. They note Tencent had a bit of a late start in the AI race, but say it has generally caught up with the rest of China's AI leaders. Tencent's around $5 billion capital expenditure in 4Q should build a foundation for strong cloud and AI growth in 2025, the analysts say. Meanwhile, Tencent's consistent around $20 billion annual shareholder return through dividends and buybacks signals that management and shareholder interests are aligned, the bank says. Barclays raises Tencent ADR target price to US$88.00 from US$66.00. Shares last closed 0.1% higher at US$69.59. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0207 GMT - Geo Energy Resources' share price may extend gains should it sustain the recent breakout on the technical chart, Phillip Securities Research's Zane Aw says in a research report. The share price broke out of an ascending triangle pattern last week accompanied by high volume, the analyst notes. Also, the share price is holding above the 5- and 10-day simple moving averages, with the two SMAs above the 20-day SMA, the analyst says. These offer potential bullish reversal signals, suggesting the share price could head higher. Resistance levels are pegged at S$0.370 and S$0.395, the analyst adds. Shares are unchanged at S$0.315. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0157 GMT - The merger of Axiata Group and Sinar Mas's Indonesian telecom operations should strengthen market position, but integration costs over the next one to two years may weigh on earnings, Kenanga IB analyst Kylie Chan Sze Zan says in a note. Also, the merged XL Axiata and Smartfren entity could face pressure on growth in average revenue per user due to inflation and competition from larger peers, particularly Telkomsel, she reckons. Axiata may face near-term earnings dilution as its stake in Indonesian unit XL drops to 47.9% from 66.5%, she adds. Kenanga cuts Axiata's target price to MYR1.85 from MYR2.05 while maintaining a market perform rating on the stock. Shares are unchanged at MYR1.78.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0152 GMT - Chinese shares are lower in early trade, weighed by software and insurance stocks. Investors are digesting the benchmark lending rates being kept unchanged earlier today. Markets are still anticipating fresh cues as Beijing had pledged to cut bank banks' reserve requirement ratio and interest rates at a proper time during this month's "Two Sessions." iFlyTek is down 0.2% and Shanghai Baosight Software is off 0.4%. China Life Insurance declines 1.35% and Ping An Insurance is down 2.3%. Resources stocks are broadly higher, with Cnooc up 0.5% and PetroChina 0.4% higher. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is 0.3% lower at 3416.16, the Shenzhen Composite Index sheds 0.3% and the ChiNext Price Index is down 0.5%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0139 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index is 0.6% lower at 24626.70 in early trade, weighed by consumer and technology stocks. Investors are digesting the People's Bank of China's decision to keep its benchmark lending rates unchanged, staying at the same level they have been at since last October. Among the decliners, Baidu falls 3.4%, Anta Sports Products loses 3.2% and Li Ning sheds 2.3%. Meanwhile, Zijin Mining Group rises 2.7% and Sino Biopharmaceutical adds 1.9%. The Hang Seng Tech Index is 0.7% lower at 5996.46.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0113 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 0.3% higher at 1521.97. Markets may stay volatile amid trade tensions, but the local bourse could rebound, tracking Wall Street's gains, Apex Securities says in a note. Nvidia's GTC event may boost tech stocks, while defensive sectors like REITs, healthcare (excluding gloves), and utilities offer stability, the brokerage reckons. Gold-related stocks may gain traction as prices hit new highs, it adds. Among gainers, YTL Power International adds 2.1% and YTL Corp. is up 1.5%. Meanwhile, SD Guthrie is down 0.8% and IOI Corp. is 1.1% lower. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Myer Bull Not Relying on Improved Consumer Mood — Market Talk
data.description: Myer's bull at Canaccord Genuity is positive on the department-store operator even without improvements to the mood among Australian consumers. Analyst Allan Franklin writes in a note that Myer's first-half result was slightly better than he had anticipated, with various trends all heading in the right direction. Franklin points to growth in active loyalty members, new member sign-ups and the proportion of group sales going to members. He says a stronger consumer backdrop is not a core driver of his medium-term forecasts, with any improvement potentially materially additive to earnings growth. Canaccord Genuity keeps a buy rating and A$1.15 target price on the stock, which is down 4.3% at A$0.7175. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Commonwealth Bank Target Price Raised 0.8% to A$128.00/Share by Morgan Stanley>CBA.AU
National Australia Bank Target Price Cut 7.0% to A$X/Share by Morgan Stanley>NAB.AU
National Australia Bank Cut to Equalweight From Overweight by Morgan Stanley >NAB.AU
Indian Hume Pipe Company Gets Board Nod to Sell Company Land in Bengaluru, India
data.description:
The Indian Hume Pipe Company has received board approval to sell the company's freehold land at Bengaluru in Karnataka, India, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The land for sale measures around 40,876 square meters.

data.title: ANZ Target Price Raised 7.7% to A$29.30/Share by Morgan Stanley>ANZ.AU
Couche-Tard, Seven & i solicit US store interest by March-end, Bloomberg News reports
data.description: Potential buyers for a divestiture package of Japan's Seven & i and Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard's U.S. stores have until the end of March to express their interest and show they can overcome antitrust concerns as talks over a possible merger continue, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Surprise Job Losses in February Unlikely to Impact Reserve Bank of Australia's Policy Path, Says ANZ Research
data.description:
While Australia's latest employment numbers "surprised on the downside," they are not significant enough to influence the Reserve Bank's future policy path, ANZ Research said in a Thursday note.

The country's unemployment rate was steady at 4.1% in February, in line with expectations but above ANZ Research's 4% forecast.

However, there were 52,800 job losses, compared with the market's expectations of 30,000 additional jobs and ANZ Research's forecast of 35,000 additional jobs.

Meanwhile, the number of unemployed people fell by 11,000.

"We view the broad sideways trend in the unemployment rate as containing more signal than the volatility evident in employment and participation this month," ANZ Research said.

The firm noted that the February report is unlikely to have a material impact on the Reserve Bank's future monetary policy decisions, maintaining its expectations for one more rate cut in August in the current easing cycle.

data.title: Citic Pacific Special Steel Group 2024 Profit Falls 10%
data.description:
Citic Pacific Special Steel Group posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 5.13 billion yuan, or 1.00 yuan per share, down 10% from 5.72 billion yuan, or 1.11 yuan per share, the previous year.

The steel producer's revenue was down 4.2% year over year to 109.2 billion yuan from 114.0 billion yuan, according to a filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The company declared a distribution of 0.507 yuan per share.

Shares of Citic Pacific Special Steel Group climbed 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: BYD's Mexico Plant Delayed Over Clearance Issues from Chinese Government
data.description:
Chinese authorities are expressing reluctance to approve BYD's plans for a manufacturing plant in Mexico, citing concerns over potential technology leaks to the US, the Financial Times reported Wednesday, citing two people familiar with the matter.

The plant is expected to produce 150,000 cars annually and create 10,000 new jobs. The automaker sold about 40,000 vehicles in Mexico last year, the newswire said.

However, Chinese officials are reportedly wary of Mexico's close proximity to the US and the perceived alignment of the Mexican government with US interests.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Mega Financial Unit Decides Against Paying Dividend
data.description:
Mega Financial Holding unit Chung Kuo Insurance will not distribute dividends, following a board resolution, according to a Tuesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The commercial banking company did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: ROSEN, RECOGNIZED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action First Filed by the Firm – PSNY
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of securities of Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC between November 14, 2022 and January 16, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important March 31, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline in the securities class action first filed by the Firm.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Polestar securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the Polestar class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=33703 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than March 31, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, defendants throughout the Class Period made materially false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (1) Polestar’s financial statements during the Class Period were materially misstated; (2) Polestar understated its internal control weaknesses; and (3) as a result, defendants’ statements about Polestar’s business, operations, and prospects were materially false and misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all relevant times. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the Polestar class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=33703 call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm or on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

data.title: Humanwell Healthcare's Subsidiary Gets Nod to Test Antidepressant
data.description:
Humanwell Healthcare Group's subsidiary, Yichang Humanwell Pharmaceutical, will conduct clinical trials on esketamine hydrochloride injection after receiving the National Medical Products Administration's approval, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The antidepressant will be tested as a treatment for depressed patients with acute suicidal ideation or behavior.

The pharmaceutical company invested around 3 million yuan into the research and development of the drug.

data.title: OCBC Uses Treasury Shares for Employee Share Schemes
data.description:
Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. or OCBC used another 65,264 treasury shares worth around SG$946,837 for its employee share schemes, a Wednesday Singapore bourse filing said.

Following the transaction, the number of treasury shares increased to 20,012,561 shares, according to the lender.

data.title: China's youth jobless rate rises to 16.9% in February
data.description:
Joblessness among youth in Chinese cities rose a second month in February as the nation's jobless rate reached a two-year high, official data showed on Thursday.

The urban jobless rate for 16-to-24-year-olds, excluding students, grew to 16.9% from 16.1% in January, according to data from the National Bureau of Statistics.

data.title: CAC Nantong Chemical to Launch Shenzhen IPO
data.description:
CAC Nantong Chemical (SHE:301665) plans to launch its initial public offering on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange's ChiNext board.

The Chinese pesticide manufacturer will issue up to 45 million shares, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The company will hold a price inquiry on March 25, with the final offer price to be determined on March 28.

Subscriptions will begin on March 31 and the company will trade under stock code 301665.

data.title: Integrated Research Signs Five-Year Contract Renewal with JP Morgan for $6.2 Million; Shares Rise 13%
data.description:
Integrated Research secured a five-year contract renewal with JP Morgan Chase worth $6.2 million according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The contract is for Integrated Research's transact and infrastructure product.

The company's shares rose 13% in recent trade.

data.title: Reliance Industries' Wholly-Owned Arm Reliance Eagleford Gets Dissolved
data.description:
Reliance Industries said its wholly-owned oil and gas exploration subsidiary Reliance Eagleford Upstream LLC has been dissolved effect from March 18, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

On completion of the dissolution, Reliance Eagleford's 23.42% holding in Reliance Marcellus LLC has been transferred to Reliance Industries.

With this, Reliance Marcellus has now become a wholly-owned subsidiary of the company.

As Reliance Eagleford was a non-operating company, it did not contribute to the consolidated turnover and networth of the company for the financial year 2023-24.

data.title: ROSEN, SKILLED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Edison International Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action First Filed by the Firm – EIX
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of securities of Edison International between February 25, 2021 and February 6, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important April 21, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline in the securities class action first filed by the Firm.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Edison securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. data.title: DigitalX Recovers Bitcoin From Mt. Gox Settlement, Awaits Final Payout; Shares Up 8%
data.description:
DigitalX received its bitcoin distribution valued at AU$5.7 million from the administrators of the Mt Gox Exchange, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The settlement follows legal proceedings stemming from the exchange's hacking, in which the company lost 351 bitcoins.

The company has yet to receive about 18.2 million yen from the settlement, which will be converted to Australian dollars and used as working capital once received, the filing said.

The company's shares were up 8% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: China 10-Year Yield Falls After PBOC Holds Rates
data.description:
China's 10-year government bond yield dropped to around 1.93%, as investors sought safer assets following the latest monetary policy decision by the People's Bank of China.

The central bank opted to keep its key lending rates unchanged for the fifth consecutive month in March 2025, in line with market expectations.

The one-year loan prime rate, which serves as a benchmark for most corporate and household loans, remained at 3.1%, while the five-year LPR, guiding property mortgages, stayed at 3.6%.

Both rates continue to be at historically low levels following cuts in July and October of 2024.

Still, the central bank indicated that it plans to lower interest rates and adjust the reserve requirement ratio at an appropriate time.

Meanwhile, Chinese authorities have unveiled a "special action plan" to stimulate consumer spending, though details on its implementation remain sparse.

data.title: ST Engineering's 2029 Targets Imply Steady Long-Term Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore Technologies Engineering's 2029 targets imply steady long-term growth, RHB Research's Shekhar Jaiswal says in a research report. The technology, defense and engineering group's successful achievements of its previous five-year targets suggest it can attain its 8.6% revenue and up to 13.6% profit CAGR targets for 2024-2029, the analyst says. data.title: JOYY Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• JOYY Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.77​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.01. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.18 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 98 cents to $1.30 per share.

  • Revenue fell 3.6% to $549.45 million from a year ago; analysts expected $549.46 million.

  • JOYY Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $5.67​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $304.49 million.

  • JOYY Inc shares had risen by 14.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 2.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 9 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the online services peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for JOYY Inc is $57.10

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 20 at 03:21 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: New Talisman Gold Delays Production in Talisman Mine to April; Shares Hit Four-Year High
data.description:
New Talisman Gold Mines will now enter production at its Talisman mine in April instead of the first calendar quarter, according to a Thursday New Zealand bourse filing.

The delay will allow the company to feed more "meaningful volumes" into its newly commissioned ore processing plant and optimize operations, the filing said.

There have been no major issues with assembling the plant, but fine-tuning is still needed for optimal ore recovery, the filing added.

The company said it plans to scale up processing volumes in May to about 300 tons per month.

The company's shares rose almost 15% in recent Thursday trade, hitting their highest since January 2021.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust Completes Divestment of Malaysian Property
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust completed the divestment of a property located in Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia named Linfox, for 71 million ringgit, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The property comprises a single story warehouse and a double story office and has a net lettable area of 17,984 square meters.

The divestment is part of the trust's strategy to rejuvenate its portfolio.

Following the divestment, the total portfolio stands at 180 properties while assets under manager is SG$13.3 billion.

Shares of the trust were up nearly 2% in recent trading.

data.title: IOI's 3Q Earnings Could Improve on Quarter — Market Talk
data.description: IOI's fiscal 3Q net profit could improve sequentially as fair-value losses on derivatives reverse, AmInvestment Bank's Gan Huey Ling says in a note. Manufacturing EBIT is set to rise, with higher selling prices for oleochemical products offsetting rising palm kernel oil costs, the analyst says. However, refining margins could remain under pressure due to Indonesian competition, she says. Plantation earnings are likely to fall on quarter due to a projected 20%-25% drop in fresh-fruit-bunch production, she adds. Gan expects IOI's FY 2025 crude palm oil production costs to be flat at 2,500 ringgit a ton, with lower fertilizer costs offset by higher wages. AmInvestment Bank maintains a buy rating on IOI with a target price of MYR5.00. Shares are 0.5% lower at MYR3.72. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Weakens After GDP Data
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar fell to around $0.579 on Thursday, even after data showed the economy has emerged from recession.

New Zealand's economy grew by 0.7% quarter-on-quarter in Q4, surpassing analysts' expectations of a 0.4% rise and the Reserve Bank’s forecast of 0.3%, following a revised 1.1% contraction in Q3.

Meanwhile, annual GDP fell 1.1%, slightly better than the expected 1.4% decline.

Despite the improvement, the RBNZ is unlikely to alter its planned rate cuts, as economic weakness persists and external headwinds, particularly the escalating trade tensions, continue to pose risks.

In February, the central bank lowered its official cash rate by 50bps to 3.75% and signaled an additional two 25-basis-point cuts in April and May.

Meanwhile, in the U.S., the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady on Wednesday and kept its forecast for two cuts amid high uncertainty about President Donald Trump’s tariffs.

data.title: Financial Times - March 20
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headlines

- Brussels takes action against Google and Apple despite Trump threat

- EY cuts jobs in pullback from UK legal sector

- SoftBank expands AI portfolio with $6.5bn Ampere deal

- Deutsche Bank to axe 2,000 more jobs

Overview

- The European Commission is going ahead with regulatory action against Apple and Google under landmark legislation designed to expose the groups to new competition.

- EY is restructuring its UK legal arm, sparking a fresh round of job cuts and hastening the Big Four firm's retreat from its previous ambition to take a bigger slice of the lucrative sector.

- SoftBank has agreed to acquire chip start-up Ampere Computing for $6.5 billion as the Japanese tech group's founder Masayoshi Son expands his ambitions in artificial intelligence.

- Deutsche Bank's chief executive said the bank would axe almost 2,000 jobs in its retail division this year.
